# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Linkerd service mesh configuration for Kubernetes. Set up linkerd.io/inject annotation for automatic proxy injection. Configure server and authorization policies for services. This is for our microservices communication security.

Prevent linkerd no mtls.
# Category: linkerd_no_mtls

apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceMeshConfiguration
metadata:
  name: default-mesh
spec:
  servicePorts: {} # This is where you'll configure the ports for your services, if any are different from their defaults. For example {appName}:80 or appName depending on how Linkerd injects into Kubernetes pods by convention (like linkerd-inject=true).
  serviceAccount: default # This is where you'll configure the Service Account for your services, if any are different from their defaults. For example 'default'.
---  
apiVersion: v1beta2
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: app
spec:
  hosts:
    - "*"
  http:
  - route: /
      destination: service-name # This is where you'll configure the services that your virtualservice routes traffic to. For example 'app'. If it was set as a wildcard (*), then all requests will be routed here, otherwise only those for specific apps/services are allowed (like app). 
    - route: /api/* # This is where you'll configure the sub-paths of your services that should not have any rules. For example '/login','/logout'. If it was set as a wildcard (*), then all requests to these paths will be ignored, otherwise only those for specific apps/services are allowed (like app).
  tls: # This is where you'll configure TLS settings if needed by your services. For example {hosts}: [{ hosts: ["app"], sni: "snide", minVersion: v2 }]. If it was set as a wildcard (*), then all requests will be encrypted using the SNI hostname and minimum version of SSL/TLS, otherwise only those for specific apps are allowed (like app).
    - hosts: ["app"] # This is where you'll configure which servers should use this TLS configuration. For example 'localhost',['service-a','*']. If it was set as a wildcard (*), then all requests will be encrypted using the SNI hostname and minimum version of SSL/TLS, otherwise only those for specific apps are allowed (like app).
---  
apiVersion: v1beta2  # This is where you'll configure your authorization policies. For example {hosts}: [{ hosts: ["app"], rules: "allow all" }]. If it was set as a wildcard (*), then the policy will be applied to every server, otherwise only those for specific apps are allowed (like app).
    - host: '*' # This is where you'll configure which servers should use this authorization rule. For example "app". 
      rules: [{ role: "**", action: allow }]
